definitions.text: Lack of difficulty; the ability to do something easily.
examples: He played the ukelele with ease.
The ease of lifting the weight, despite lack of skill, came from her pure strength.

definitions.text: Comfort, a state or quality lacking unpleasantness, particularly:
examples: She enjoyed the ease of living in a house where the servants did all the work.
The pension set her mind at ease.

definitions.text: Freedom from pain, hardship, and annoyance, sometimes idleness, sloth.
examples: She enjoyed the ease of living in a house where the servants did all the work.

definitions.text: To free (something) from pain, worry, agitation, etc.
examples: He eased his conscience by confessing.
The provision of extra staff eased their workload.
